title: Aperture Health — Trial plan & procurement touchpoints
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Nora Jenkins <nora.jenkins@aperturehealth.com>, Tom Delgado <tom.delgado@aperturehealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Aperture Health — Trial plan & procurement touchpoints

Hi Nora / Tom —

Thanks again for taking the time today. Wanted to put a short recap in writing and call out the action items we agreed on so everyone is aligned. If I've missed anything, tell me and I'll update the MAP.

Key pain / goals you shared:
- Legal/regulatory teams require full audit logs + request-level retention controls for any PII-exposed inference.
- Average latency target for clinical-coding use is <= 200ms p50 for 512-token prompts; spikes are sensitive during peak hours.
- Budget: aim to keep hosting/op costs under existing vendor spend for the pilot window.

Proposed pilot scope (what we discussed):
- 6-week technical pilot: common EHR snippet anonymization -> structured-coding suggestions -> human-in-the-loop validation.
- Up to 5 concurrent users in testing; production load unknown today.
- Model: Redwood curated LLM (quantized variant) in a private VPC deployment.

Mutual Action Plan (draft milestones / owners / dates):
1) Trial kickoff & environment access — Aperture IT to provide VPC peering info and a test dataset (anonymized) by 2026-04-26 — Owner: Tom Delgado (Aperture).
2) Security & compliance Q to be completed — Redwood to send SOC2 evidence + retention controls doc by 2026-04-23 — Owner: Ben Carter (Redwood); Aperture to share any additional compliance checklist by 2026-04-28.
3) Integration test (connect, inference, KV-caching validation) — target 2026-05-06 — Owner: Monica Patel (Redwood) / Aperture dev lead TBD.
4) Latency & correctness smoke test (sample prompts + eval set) — target 2026-05-13 — Owner: Karthik Iyer (Redwood) / Nora Jenkins (Aperture).
5) Procurement & T&C alignment — Aperture procurement to send redlines or PO intent by 2026-05-20 — Owner: Aperture Procurement.

Attachments: I attached a succinct MAP PDF and a one-page terms summary that highlights data handling and uptime.

Immediate asks for Aperture: please confirm the procurement contact (name & email) and whether the pilot dataset can include small samples of sensitive codes (we'll treat them as covered data and store only encrypted KV cache).

Next step: I'll follow up on Friday with a calendar invite for a brief kickoff once we have the VPC peering details.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0142
[attachment: Aperture_MAP_v1.pdf (application/pdf)]
[attachment: Redwood_Terms_Summary_2026-04-19.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)]

From: Nora Jenkins <nora.jenkins@aperturehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Delgado <tom.delgado@aperturehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Aperture Health — Trial plan & procurement touchpoints

Ben —

Thanks for the clear recap. This aligns with what we discussed. Quick answers:
- Procurement lead will be Alyssa Park (alyssa.park@aperturehealth.com). I'll introduce her on the procurement thread today.
- We can provide a 500-record anonymized sample of EHR notes by 2026-04-27; it will include diagnosis/procedure codes with direct identifiers stripped.
- Regarding latency: our clinical ops team will validate p50/p95 targets against 512-token prompts; if there is a significant delta on p95 we may request a fallback routing policy for critical workflows.

Two notes from Legal: they want a short summary of how retention is handled for prompt text and how keys are rotated if we proceed with a private deployment. Can you add that to the terms summary?

I'll loop Alyssa and procurement after I finish a quick internal sync.

Nora
Nora Jenkins
Chief Product Officer | Aperture Health
Nora.Jenkins@aperturehealth.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Park <alyssa.park@aperturehealth.com>, Nora Jenkins <nora.jenkins@aperturehealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Tom Delgado <tom.delgado@aperturehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Intro + brief on procurement asks (Alyssa)

Hi Alyssa —

Great to meet you (virtually). Per Nora, I'm attaching a one-page procurement summary that calls out: pricing model for the 6-week pilot, proposed SLAs for Dedicated/private, and a short data handling appendix that addresses prompt retention and key rotation.

Quick highlights for procurement review:
- Pilot pricing: flat pilot fee + overage tiers if we cross the concurrency threshold. Exact numbers in the doc.
- T&Cs: we are open to limited redlines on data retention and audit access; anything beyond standard enterprise changes will require legal review.
- PO intent timeline: if Aperture can provide intent by 2026-05-20 we can prioritize a managed onramp the following week.

Action for Redwood: Monica will own the technical kickoff and integration work. Karthik will lead performance tuning and the eval set.

I'll follow up with a calendar invite for a 30-min procurement sync (include Alyssa + Tom + our revenue ops) for 2026-04-23 or 2026-04-26 — which day works better for you?

Best —
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
[attachment: Redwood_Terms_Summary_2026-04-19.docx]

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Aperture Health — Trial plan & procurement touchpoints

Ben / Karthik —

A couple of engineering notes ahead of the kickoff:
- We'll request a short VPN/VPC test window and credentials from Aperture; if peering is delayed we can run through a proxy mode with synthetic data first to validate latency and KV cache behavior.
- For the latency target: we'll profile both p50 and p95 and recommend a fallback routing policy if p95 exceeds 800ms under synthetic peak. That fallback would route to a lower-latency but lower-cost variant for non-critical queries.
- I'll prepare a short checklist for the integration call (connectivity, auth credentials, sample payloads, metric hooks).

Sending the checklist by EOD Monday.

Monica
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.com

title: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Hi Alex —

Hope you had a good week. As Cloudscale's annual renewal is approaching (term ends 2026-06-30), I wanted to kick off a short renewal QBR to align on capacity, the proposed uplift, and a potential 12-month term extension with a committed capacity tier. High-level goals for the meeting: 

- Review usage trends and token burn (last 6 months)
- Discuss the +30% capacity uplift proposal to reduce queueing for peak workloads
- Walk through a 12-mo term amendment and associated pricing/discount tiers
- Agree on next steps and a mutual action plan (MAP) with owners

Proposed duration: 45 minutes. Can you confirm a couple of 45-min windows next week? I can take 4/20 morning (9–11 PT) or 4/21 afternoon (1–4 PT). If those don't work, send 2–3 alternatives and we'll follow up with a calendar invite.

I attached a draft QBR agenda and a preliminary pricing worksheet for the proposed uplift — these are internal-first but I can share a cleaned version after we confirm timing.

Key ask: if there are procurement or security folks who should join (legal, procurement, data-security), please loop them in. For context, the uplift is primarily to support Cloudscale's new ingestion pipeline (batch inference spikes) and would come with a brief onboarding slot from our Dedicated Ops team.

Thanks — looking forward to the sync.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0126

Attachment: QBR_agenda_v1.pdf (agenda)
Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Jamie Torres <jamie.torres@cloudscale.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Ben —

Thanks for the heads up and for the draft materials. We want to get ahead of the renewal discussion. 4/20 at 10:00 PT works for me; Jamie can confirm in a follow-up.

Two quick items for the call: 
1) Can you include a short pricing comparison for a 6‑month vs 12‑month commit (I know you sent a worksheet, but a one‑slide comparison will help our procurement).
2) We have an internal security reviewer (Lina Ramos) who will likely ask for details on data retention/audit logs — can Priya be prepared to speak to retention and Private deployment options?

Also — we may be asking for a short credit to offset some overage from a prior quarter while negotiating the uplift. Not a formal ask yet, but want to flag it so it's on the table.

Alex
VP, Platform Engineering
Cloudscale Dynamics

On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alex —
> 
> Hope you had a good week. As Cloudscale's annual renewal is approaching (term ends 2026-06-30), I wanted to kick off a short renewal QBR...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>, Jamie Torres <jamie.torres@cloudscale.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Working notes / prep items before the 4/20 meeting (I’ll drop the finalized slide deck to Alex after the call):

- Pricing: I prepared a one‑slide comparison (6m vs 12m) and an uplift schedule tied to committed TPS and token tiers. See attached. For the credit ask Alex mentioned, we can propose a one‑time goodwill credit equal to the overage delta for Q1, contingent on signing the 12‑month uplift by 5/15.
- Security/retention: Priya — please be ready to summarize: default retention windows, audit log granularity, and Private deployment + KMS options. If Lina wants SOC2 artifacts, we can surface the SOC2 report link during the call.
- Ops/onboarding: Recommend scheduling a 2-hour onboarding slot with Dedicated Ops within 2 weeks of amendment signing — covers KV cache warmup, burst policy, and autoscale guardrails.
- MAP: I’ll own creating the mutual action plan doc in HubSpot and assign owners post-call.

I've attached the pricing slide and a draft MAP template.

Thanks team — I’ll join the 4/20 10:00 PT slot (Ben, please send the invite).

Marissa Cole
Account Director, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx (pricing slide)
Attachment: MAP_template_v1.docx (hubspot friendly checklist)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Bowman <alex.bowman@cloudscale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: QBR scheduling + capacity uplift & term change request

Alex —

Great — 4/20 10:00 PT is locked and calendar invite is out (you should have it). Agenda attached will be the working doc; we'll capture action items and publish the MAP in HubSpot within 24 hours of the meeting.

To summarize next steps before the call: 
- Marissa: bring the 6m vs 12m slide and proposed uplift pricing. (Attachment: proposed_uplift_pricing.xlsx)
- Priya: prepare SOC2/retention talking points and a short note on Private deployment options.
- Alex/Jamie: bring procurement constraints and Lina (security) if available.
- Ben: draft MAP in HubSpot, confirm DocuSign envelope for the amendment (tentative) and check on a credit proposal floor.

If Alex requests the pricing slide ahead of 4/20 for procurement review, Marissa will share a redacted PDF by EOD 4/21.

Thanks all — looking forward to it.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0126

Related links: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/CS-2026-Q2-0819
Attachment: QBR_agenda_v1.pdf (final will be updated post-call)

title: RE: April metering discrepancy — token sampling/aggregation question
preview:
From: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Hi Alex,

Hope you’re well. We pulled the April invoice and noticed that the billed token count is ~18% lower than the totals our instrumentation recorded for the same billing window. I attached a short CSV (usage_sample.csv) with a few request IDs and timestamps and the metering-report.pdf exported from our system.

Questions:
- Has Redwood changed the sampling/aggregation window or enabled any new prefix dedup / cache-related adjustments in the last 30 days?
- Could our migration on 2026-03-31 (we spun up a rollout service) have affected how requests are reported to you — we saw a burst that day and want to rule out misattribution.

This affects our month-end reconciliation and the invoice is currently in our AP queue. We’re happy to provide signed access for a request-level audit if needed. Can you confirm next steps and expected turnaround?

Thanks,
Ivy Chen
Billing & Ops
StormScale Inc.
(415) 555-0123

Attachment: usage_sample.csv (text/csv)
Attachment: metering-report.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-18T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Ivy — thanks for the note and the attachments, I’ve started a quick triage.

Short answer: no intentional change to billing logic in April that would systematically lower billed tokens for production requests. We did roll a prefix-KV cache optimization to select internal models earlier in April, but that is additive (reduces billed tokens for repeated prefixes) and would not cause unexplained increases in your side’s totals.

Next steps I propose:
1) Loop in Ben from Finance/Accounts so we can coordinate an audit run (Ben — can you pick this up?).
2) If you can sign the data-access consent (simple form), we’ll pull request-level logs for the IDs Ivy provided and compare our ingest events to your events.
3) Quick sync — 30 minutes should be enough once we have the authorization.

Ivy: I attached a short checklist of what we normally need (redwood_prelim_checklist.pdf) to speed the process. If you can confirm you authorize us to pull per-request logs for the IDs in usage_sample.csv, we’ll start a forensics export.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Head of Account Success
Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | (650) 555-0199

> On 2026-04-18 09:12, Ivy Chen wrote:
> > Hope you’re well. We pulled the April invoice and noticed that the billed token count is ~18% lower than the totals our instrumentation recorded for the same billing window.
> > ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-19T11:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling?

Hi Ivy, Alex — looping in from Finance/Measurements team. I dug into April’s rollout notes and our metering pipeline:

What I can confirm up front:
- No retroactive change to token counting rules (we count input+output tokens per request).
- On 2026-04-01 we enabled an optional prefix KV-caching optimization on a subset of model routes. That optimization reduces billed tokens only when identical prefixes and cached KV usage occur. It cannot create "extra" local tokens on your side.

Potential causes to validate:
- Double-emitted requests during your 2026-03-31 migration (retries or a proxy misconfiguration) could cause StormScale to record more client-side entries than our ingestion saw (or vice versa).
- If your instrumentation aggregates tokens with a different boundary (e.g., counting tokenization before any gateway dedup), totals will differ. We need to align sample request IDs and verify event timestamps.

Proposed approach:
1) We’ll need explicit authorization to run a request-level audit over the April window for the sample IDs. Please use the attached data-access-request-form.pdf — return with a signed initial scope and we’ll export logs within 3 business days.
2) If we find our ingestion missed requests that you recorded, we’ll propose a goodwill credit for confirmed missed ingestion events.
3) If the difference stems from differing tokenization boundaries or local duplication on your side, we’ll share a mapping document to align counting rules going forward.

Ivy — are you available for a 30-min troubleshooting call tomorrow (2026-04-20) at 10:00 PT? Alex — I’ll take point on the export once we have the signed scope.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Operations
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachment: data-access-request-form.pdf (application/pdf)

> On 2026-04-18 10:03, Alex Martinez wrote:
> > Ivy — thanks for the note and the attachments, I’ve started a quick triage.
> > ...


From: Ivy Chen <ivy.chen@stormscale.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: billing@stormscale.ai
Date: 2026-04-22T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Discrepancy in April metering — token sampling? — authorization attached

Ben / Alex,

Thanks — attached is the signed consent and the DocuSign link for our AP manager to authorize the audit (docusign-stormscale-consent.pdf). We also re-ran our local sample and found that during the 2026-03-31 migration window a proxy misconfiguration caused a small set of requests to be retried at the gateway level before reaching our application. That produced duplicate entries in our internal instrumentation for about ~0.9% of sampled requests.

We still see ~17% remaining discrepancy after accounting for duplicates, so please proceed with the export. If your audit shows missed ingestion on Redwood’s side, we will expect an invoice credit for confirmed missed events. If it turns out to be counting boundary differences, let’s get a short doc with the canonical counting rules so we can align for May onwards.

I’m available tomorrow for the 30-min call at 10:00 PT.

Best,
Ivy Chen
Billing & Ops
StormScale Inc.

Attachment: docusign-stormscale-consent.pdf (application/pdf)

> > ...

title: Pilot credit allocation and trial extension request - NovaForge
preview:
From: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the quick product demo last week — the team at NovaForge (we're an accelerator cohort company) is excited about Redwood's latency and cost controls. We're preparing a 6-week evaluation across two internal apps and would like to request: 1) a 60-day trial extension on the hosted plan, and 2) a $10,000 promotional credit to cover pilot usage and a small production-like load.

High level:
- Goal: validate inference cost at 2k TPS over short burst loads, evaluate model variants, and run our prompt-eval suite.
- Timeline: start in early May; run for ~6 weeks.
- Key success metrics: < 250ms median latency and < $0.02/token average cost for baseline prompts.

I've attached our pilot plan and a short usage sketch. Can you let me know what information you need from us and the typical approval cadence for programs like this? If helpful, Elena from Foundry Labs (copied) can vouch for the accelerator process and timing.

Thanks,
Samir Gupta
LaunchPad Ventures (portfolio rep for NovaForge)
samir@launchpad.co
+1 415-555-0123

Attachment: NovaForge-Pilot-Plan.pdf (PDF, 1.2 MB)

To: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Samir — really appreciate the clear plan and the context from Elena.

Short answer: we can usually support a pilot extension + credits for accelerator-backed evaluations. To scope it correctly, can you share an estimated token volume per day (or peak QPS) and whether NovaForge needs dedicated capacity or hosted API? Also: are any of the workloads PII-sensitive (if so we can route to a Private trial).

If you can fill this short form it will speed the review: https://redwood.app/pilot-request-form (1–2 mins).

Looping in Ben Carter on sales and Lauren Bishop from Customer Success to help land the offer and MOU. Ben — do you have a customary credit band and conditional terms you prefer for accelerator pilots?

Next steps I suggest:
- Samir completes the form + usage estimate (I attached a small spreadsheet we use)
- Ben/CS propose a conditional credit allocation and milestone success criteria
- We issue a pilot MOU (DocuSign) and enable the extension once signed

Thanks — I'll track this in HubSpot and mark as P1 for accelerator pipeline.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0199

Attachment: NovaForge-usage-estimate.xlsx (XLSX, stub)

To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Ava / Lauren —

I reviewed the pilot plan and usage sketch. Recommendation for a clean, low-friction handoff:
- Offer $8,000 in promotional credits (vs. $10k requested) tied to a 60-day extension on the hosted plan.
- Conditions: single-account usage, usage cap of 50M tokens total for the pilot, and sign the pilot MOU within 10 business days.
- Success Milestone: if NovaForge hits the latency and cost targets within the 6-week run, we convert to a Standard trial with optional Dedicated evaluation (SLA terms to be discussed).

Rationale: the requested $10k is fine but $8k keeps our average program burn reasonable for cohort-backed pilots while still covering robust evaluation. If Foundry wants to underwrite any overages we can do $10k and note sponsor contribution.

I'll create the HubSpot deal (DEAL-2026-0147) and draft the MOU. Lauren — can CS prepare a short onboarding checklist and a workspace for testing?

Ben Carter
Sales, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

title: RFI: Deployment Isolation Map, Egress Controls, and High-Level Threat Annotations
preview:
From: Jules Martin <jules.martin@wavefin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Wavefin Security <security@wavefin.com>
Date: 2026-04-19T09:13:00-07:00
Subject: RFI: Deployment isolation and threat annotations - Wavefin

Hi Ben / Vanessa,

Thanks for taking this RFI. As part of our vendor security review, Wavefin needs a concise package describing how Redwood isolates tenant workloads, controls egress, and what high-level threats you analyze for production inference deployments. Specifically we are looking for: 

- A diagram showing control plane, inference zones, sidecar/proxy placement, network ACLs, and egress gateways for your Hosted and Private (VPC) deployments.
- Short annotations on plausible threat paths (e.g., cross-tenant inference data leakage, KV cache contamination, sidecar compromise) and mitigations.
- Where applicable, evidence pointers (config names, logs, or artifacts) that we can reference in our risk assessment.

We're on a tight timeline: can you provide a written response and diagrams by EOD 2026-04-20 PT? If helpful, we can schedule a 30-min call to walk the diagrams.

Thanks,
Jules
Jules Martin
Security Program Manager, Wavefin Financial

--
Attachment: Request_RFI_Wavefin_20260416.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jules Martin <jules.martin@wavefin.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-04-19T13:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: RFI: Deployment isolation and threat annotations - Wavefin

Ben / team — quick technical notes to include in the final package. Quoting Ben's planned deliverables below.

On 2026-04-19 11:02, Ben Carter wrote:
> Planned deliverables:
> - deployment-isolation-map-v1.svg
> - threat-annotations-v1.pdf
> - security-evidence.zip

Additions / clarifications (for threat-annotations and evidence index):
1) Isolation model (short bullet for diagram):
   - Control plane (Redwood-managed) lives in a distinct VPC/region with strict peering to customer Dedicated/Private nodes. Control-plane components are zero-trust: mTLS + per-service identities; no cross-tenant data stored in control plane.
   - Data plane (inference workers) are pinned to tenancy boundaries. Hosted: logical multitenancy with per-request tenant id, KV cache namespace separation and per-tenant memory isolation enforced at the runtime process level. Private/VPC: network-enforced tenant isolation via subnets and security groups.

2) Egress and gateway model:
   - Egress gateways (customer-configurable for Private) are colocated at the cluster edge; support NAT + logging to customer log sinks. Hosted deployments route egress through our hardened, monitored egress fleet with allowlists and TLS interception for traffic scanning as agreed.

3) KV cache / model state threat note:
   - Threat: adversarial prompt or model-stealing attempts via crafted inputs leading to cross-request leakage.
   - Mitigations: per-tenant KV namespaces, eviction and TTL policies, runtime mem-scrub on model swap, and audit hooks for unusual cache hits. Evidence: runtime config file named runtime/cache_namespaces.yaml (included in security-evidence.zip).

4) Sidecar compromise / lateral movement:
   - Threat: a compromised sidecar attempts to reach other tenant workers or control plane.
   - Mitigations: eBPF-based network policies; strict outbound firewall rules; process-level seccomp profiles; RBAC for sidecar control plane calls; periodic canarying on sidecar images. Evidence: firewall ruleset snippet (included).

5) Recommended snippet for the annotations doc: a short table of risk, likelihood, impact, and primary controls. Keep it high level for customer RFI.

Attaching the preliminary evidence bundle for indexing. I'll refine the threat descriptions once Marcus confirms runtime nuance.

Irene Choi
Senior Security Engineer
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: security-evidence.zip (zip stub containing: runtime/cache_namespaces.yaml, firewall-rules-snippet.txt, audit-log-sample.json)

title: Order Form / MSA — Term, Auto-Renew & Termination for Convenience
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software>, procurement@greenline.software
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Draft order form + MSA redlines — term & auto-renew items

Hi Laura / Greenline procurement team,

Thanks again for the demo last week — appreciate the thoughtful questions. Attached are our current suggested redlines for the Order Form and the MSA focusing on the Term, Auto-Renew, and Termination provisions. The attachments include a short summary of proposed language in the cover doc (MSA_term_comments.pdf).

High-level items we intend to propose:
- Term length aligned with Order Form (12 months) with one (1) automatic renewal unless either party gives notice within the notice window.
- Proposed notice window: 120 days prior to renewal (we understand Greenline requested longer; open to discussion).
- Termination for Convenience: Redwood is willing to accept mutual termination for convenience, subject to a pro rata refund for prepaid fees and a 30-day wind-down period to allow transition.
- Termination for Cause: standard cure periods (30 days) retained.

Please review and let us know the must-haves for procurement — specifically on: notice window length, whether termination for convenience is acceptable at all, and whether you require an explicit auto-renew opt-out mechanism (email vs. portal).

Attachments: Redlines_OrderForm_v3.docx, MSA_term_comments.pdf

Next steps: If you can provide Greenline's required notice window and any internal approvals needed for Termination for Convenience by end of day Tue (4/20), we can have Legal provide a final pass on the redline language by Thu.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132

----


From: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@greenline.software
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 13:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft order form + MSA redlines — term & auto-renew items

Ben — thanks for sending. Procurement and legal reviewed these highlights. A couple of key constraints from our side:

1) Notice window: Greenline requires at least a 180-day non-renewal notice for any contract auto-renewal with production workloads; 120 days is too short for our procurement cycles and budget planning.

2) Termination for Convenience: Our preference is to avoid unilateral termination for convenience by either party for production services. We can consider mutual termination for convenience only, and only if there is an explicit transition and SLA-credit framework that protects Greenline's live deployments.

3) Auto-renew opt-out: If auto-renew remains, procurement requires written notice via email to procurement@greenline.software and a confirmation reply from Redwood; a portal-only opt-out is insufficient for audit records.

I've attached our internal requirements spreadsheet (Greenline_requirements.xlsx) with the redlines we would like to see.

Can you share Redwood's rationale for a 120-day window? If we can meet in the middle at 150 days, that might be workable, but need to confirm with Finance and Legal.

Best,
Laura Cheng
Head of Procurement | Greenline Software
laura.cheng@greenline.software

----
On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 09:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Laura / Greenline procurement team,
> 
> Thanks again for the demo last week — appreciate the thoughtful questions. Attached are our current suggested redlines for the Order Form and the MSA focusing on the Term, Auto-Renew, and Termination provisions....



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software>, procurement@greenline.software
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft order form + MSA redlines — term & auto-renew items

Laura — appreciate the clarity and the attached requirements. Quick internal update: Monica and I chatted with Finance and our CS lead about transition risk. A couple of thoughts on compromise so we can keep momentum on the POC-to-production timeline: 

- Notice window: Redwood can agree to 150 days in good faith to bridge the gap between 120/180.
- Termination for Convenience: We are comfortable with mutual termination for convenience language; Redwood can accept unilateral termination for convenience only with the following guardrails: 1) 60-day advance notice; 2) pro rata refund for any prepaid fees; 3) assistance during a 30-day wind-down (reasonable cooperation). If Greenline cannot accept unilateral termination at all, we can make it mutual-only.
- Auto-renew opt-out: We agree that an email to procurement@greenline.software is an acceptable opt-out method provided Redwood issues a confirmation receipt email and retains the audit trail for 3 years.

Monica / Karthik — can Legal confirm whether 'mutual-only termination for convenience' + 150-day notice is acceptable from our risk posture? If Legal prefers unilateral with guardrails, we'll flag that to the customer.

Ben

----
On Sat, Apr 18, 2026 at 01:02 PM Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for sending. Procurement and legal reviewed these highlights. A couple of key constraints from our side:...



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software>, procurement@greenline.software
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Draft order form + MSA redlines — term & auto-renew items

Team / Laura — I reviewed Ben's proposal and Greenline's constraints. Legal notes and proposed language attached (Legal_redline_MSATerm_20260418.docx). Summary of our view: 

- Acceptable compromise: 150-day non-renewal notice. We'll update the Order Form so the renewal trigger states: "Contract will renew for successive 12-month terms unless either party provides written notice at least 150 days prior to the then-current Term expiration."

- Termination for Convenience: Recommend mutual termination for convenience as the baseline. If Greenline requires the option for Redwood to terminate for convenience (we generally avoid unilateral termination for production contracts), we will accept it only with the following contract language: 1) 60 days' written notice; 2) pro rata refund of prepaid Subscription Fees; 3) Redwood will provide reasonable transition assistance for up to 30 days following termination and will cooperate with data export requests; 4) an express carve-out for any fees already invoiced and non-cancellable third-party costs.

- Opt-out audit trail: Add explicit mechanism: opt-out must be sent to procurement@greenline.software and will be acknowledged by Redwood via a confirmation email that will be retained per our records-retention policy (3 years).

- Additional ask: Add a Market Disruption clause to handle exceptional termination events (e.g., regulatory ban on specific models) — language included in the attached redline.

If this is acceptable to Greenline, I can prepare a tracked redline and a clean copy for countersignature. Ben — please confirm whether Sales is okay to present mutual-only termination as the starting position; otherwise we'll include the unilateral-with-guardrails language as Karthik suggested.

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

----
On Sun, Apr 19, 2026 at 08:40 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Laura — appreciate the clarity and the attached requirements. Quick internal update: Monica and I chatted with Finance and our CS lead about transition risk. A couple of thoughts on compromise so we can keep momentum on the POC-to-production timeline:...



From: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@greenline.software>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@greenline.software
Date: Mon, 20 Apr 2026 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft order form + MSA redlines — term & auto-renew items

Karthik / Ben — thanks for the thoughtful response and the proposed language. We are close. A couple of final points from Greenline Legal and Finance: 

- SLA / credits tied to termination: Greenline requires that any termination for convenience that impacts live services include a minimum SLA credit equal to the unfulfilled portion of the month or a negotiated credit schedule. This should be in the Order Form so Finance can reconcile invoicing.

- Market Disruption clause: We accept a narrowly tailored clause for regulatory or force majeure events, but want it to require good-faith consultation prior to invocation.

If Redwood can produce a tracked redline with the 150-day notice, mutual-only termination for convenience (with an option to switch to unilateral-with-guardrails only if Redwood has documented business need), explicit opt-out email confirmation language, and a short SLA-credit table example, Greenline will circulate internally for final sign-off and get back to you by end of week.

We also prefer DocuSign for the final signature; please route the envelope to procurement@greenline.software and me directly.

Thanks,
Laura

- Opt-out audit trail: Add explicit mechanism: opt-out must be sent to procurement@greenline.software and will be acknowledged by Redwood via a confirmation email that will be retained per our records-retention policy (3 years).

- Additional ask: Add a Market Disruption clause to handle exceptional termination events (e.g., regulatory ban on specific models) — language included in the attached redline.

If this is acceptable to Greenline, I can prepare a tracked redline and a clean copy for countersignature. Ben — please confirm whether Sales is okay to present mutual-only termination as the starting position; otherwise we'll include the unilateral-with-guardrails language as Karthik suggested.

Thanks,

title: QuickChat: volume profile, blended rate example & model premium clarifications
preview:
From: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency and routing controls look promising. We're scoping an initial production deployment and want to validate economics before we lock a commit. High level: QuickChat expects ~50M generated tokens / month after ramp (month 4+), with the following approximate mix: 60% chat (mainly streaming completion), 30% embeddings (vector store writes + similarity queries), 10% misc (reranking, short calls).

Questions:
- Can you provide a blended per-token example for that mix using both (a) standard Redwood models and (b) a scenario where we use the premium chat model for 25% of chat traffic?
- How do you treat embeddings pricing — per-token or per-vector? Any differences between standard vs premium embedding models?
- What commit tiers/discounts do you recommend for 50M/month vs 100M/month?
- Any trial or onboarding credits for the first 2 months while we ramp?

Attaching our internal usage projection for reference. Procurement is cc'd on follow-ups (SARA).

Attachments: [Attachment: QC-usage-projections.xlsx (xlsx)]

Thanks,
Ellis
Ellis Donovan
Head of Platform, QuickChat AI
ellis@quickchat.ai


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Hi Ellis — great to hear the demo was useful. I ran your projection through our pricing matrix and put together two illustrative scenarios below. These are ballpark rates to help you model TCO; I can produce a line-item quote once you confirm final model mix and commit length.

Scenario A — No commit (pay-as-you-go guidance):
- Standard chat (redwood-chat-standard): $0.00060 per generated token
- Premium chat (redwood-chat-xl): +40% uplift => $0.00084 per token
- Standard embeddings (redwood-embed-standard): $0.00025 per token-equivalent (we price embeddings per token processed during encode)
- Premium embeddings uplift: +60% => $0.00040 per token-equivalent

Scenario B — Committed 12-month Tier (50M tokens/month baseline) — list discounts applied: 12% off base per-token for total committed usage; model uplifts applied before discount. Example blended math (your mix: 60% chat standard, 30% embeddings standard, 10% premium chat):
- Weighted (no discount): 0.6*$0.00060 + 0.3*$0.00025 + 0.1*$0.00084 = $0.000522
- With 12% commit discount: $0.000522 * 0.88 = $0.000459

Notes / answers to your bullets:
- Embeddings: we bill per token encoded (not per vector); for typical QuickChat vector sizes you should expect the token-equivalent rate above. If you route writes vs query-heavy workloads we can split billing lines (writes vs queries) for clarity.
- Overage: billed at committed rate + 15% (i.e., slightly higher than your committed unit) unless we agree to a burst buffer.
- Trial credits: we can offer $15k in onboarding credits (applied to first two months) for a 12-month commit.

I've attached a short SOW and a simple blended-rate worksheet. If you want, I can build a 3-year Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) model factoring in model premium share and expected ramp.

Attachments: [Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf], [Attachment: QC-usage-projections.xlsx (updated)]

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com


From: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.procurement@quickchat.ai>
Date: Sat, 18 Apr 2026 14:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Ava — thanks for the clear math, that helps a lot. A couple of clarifying items before we share with our finance team:

- Can you confirm the premium uplift caps? If we commit to 50M/month but end up shifting 40% of chat to premium over time, will the 40% uplift be applied across the whole tier or only to the premium token subset?
- For embeddings: the token-equivalent model billing is fine, but can you produce an example that separates encode (ingest) vs query costs? That matters to our cost projection.
- SOC 2 / compliance: procurement wants a copy of your SOC 2 or point them to your compliance portal.
- Payment terms / invoice cadence: do you accept net-45 for enterprise and will the onboarding credit be applied before invoice?

Cc'ing our procurement lead Sara — Sara, feel free to add procurement questions.

Ellis


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 08:33:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — internal negotiation notes

Internal notes (do not forward) — suggested approach for QuickChat negotiation:

- Offer: 12-month commit at 50M/month baseline, 12% off list as Ava suggested. Include 2 months of onboarding credits ($15k) applied up-front.
- Premium uplift: cap at +35% for premium chat models for this deal (instead of 40%) to be more competitive; billing will be per-token split (only premium tokens charged at uplift).
- Embeddings: separate encode vs query lines; price encode at $0.00025 and queries at $0.00022 for committed customers (we can absorb some cost on queries).
- Overage: allow a 20% burst buffer monthly before overage at committed rate + 10% (helps the customer not get surprise bills during ramp).
- Margin impact: Rishi, note this reduces blended ASP by ~6 points at 50M baseline — approve or push back? If we need more margin, request a 24-month commit or raise discount to 10% and keep uplift at 40%.

I'll let Ava incorporate and send a formal offer.

Marissa
VP, Sales Operations


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ellis Donovan <ellis@quickchat.ai>, Sara Patel <sara.procurement@quickchat.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Apr 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing ask — sample blended rate for our expected mix

Ellis / Sara — thanks for the quick back-and-forth. Based on internal alignment, here is a formalized offer for QuickChat AI: 

Summary — 12-month committed agreement (Tier B):
- Baseline commit: 50M generated tokens / month (measured as generated tokens + embeddings token-equivalents)
- Discount: 12% off list per-token rates for committed baseline
- Premium model uplift: +35% applied only to premium tokens (we will meter premium model usage separately)
- Embeddings split: encode (ingest) billed at $0.00025/token; query billed at $0.00022/token for committed usage
- Overage: 20% burst buffer; beyond buffer billed at committed per-token rate + 10%
- Onboarding credits: $15,000 applied to invoice(s) in months 1-2
- Payment terms: Net-45 (approved for enterprise accounts)

Illustrative blended math (your stated mix: 60% chat standard, 30% embeddings (10% encode / 20% query), 10% premium chat):
- No discount weighted: 0.6*$0.00060 + 0.3*$0.00025 + 0.1*(0.00060*1.35) = 0.6*0.00060 + 0.3*0.00025 + 0.1*0.00081 = 0.00036 + 0.000075 + 0.000081 = $0.000516
- After 12% commit discount: $0.000516 * 0.88 = $0.000454
- Effective monthly spend at baseline (50M tokens): 50,000,000 * $0.000454 = $22,700 per month (first-order estimate before applying onboarding credits)

Next steps:
1) I can push an initial DocuSign SOW if you confirm the 50M baseline and the premium share assumptions. DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/QuickChat-SOW
2) I'll include SOC 2 link and our compliance pack in the next email for Sara.
3) If you want a 24-month commitment, we can consider tightening the discount to 14% or extending the onboarding credit.

Attachments: [Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf], [Attachment: redwood-invoice-template.pdf]

Let me know if you'd like me to join a call with finance/procurement to walk through the invoice cadence and metering model.

Best regards,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: QuickChat-SOW-draft.pdf]
[Attachment: redwood-invoice-template.pdf]

Subject: PharosRx security follow-up: SOC 2, DDQ, and trust portal access

Hi Harper —

Thanks again for taking the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on the security/compliance items you called out:

- PharosRx asks: SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letter/interim attestation, and a vendor DDQ (we heard the DDQ will be authored on your side).
- Data residency / HIPAA: Redwood supports US and EU residency; we are HIPAA-ready and can sign a BAA.
- Evidence exchange: we use a Trust Portal (secure link) for artifacts, plus S3/SFTP options for customers with procurement constraints.

My asks:
1) Can you share the DDQ document or portal invite when it’s ready? (I added Jordan to help with technical answers.)
2) Would Pharos prefer the SOC 2 Type II PDF + standard bridge letter, or do you need an expanded attestation for specific controls?
3) Timeline — what’s your deadline for receiving the report/attestation?

I attached a short post-call deck with the compliance touchpoints. Jordan and I will coordinate with our security team to get you the SOC2 package and a bridge letter by the turnaround you need.

Ben

preview:

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: Renewal + capacity questions

Hi Ben,

Quick note ahead of our renewal in May — we've attached a month-to-date usage export (pixelbay_apr_usage_summary.csv). Overall usage is up ~35% month-over-month due to a new recommender feature we launched. We're worried about overage exposure and would like to discuss:

- A recommended reserved capacity tier for the next 12 months
- Temporary burst/bucket options for expected spikes (esp. week of May 3)
- Any ROI or performance notes to justify a larger commit to our execs

Can we get a short health/ROI recap and a pricing proposal this week? Procurement is copied.

Amy Chen

Date: 2026-04-15T11:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity questions

- Can you confirm peak RPS and p50/p95 latency expectations you need to preserve?
- Is the May 3 spike expected to be sustained beyond that week?

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Date: 2026-04-16T08:37:00Z
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity questions — proposal attached

Hi Amy — attached is a concise proposal (redwood_capacity_proposal_q2.pdf) and an updated pricing worksheet (emailed_pricing_sheet_v3.xlsx). TL;DR:

- Recommendation: move to Reserved Tier B (12-mo commit) at +40% token capacity vs current average with a 20% credit on overages for the first billing quarter.
- Burst option: 10-day burst bucket (pre-purchased) sized for estimated May 3 spike at the blended reserved + burst unit rate. Burst usage does not count toward the commit.
- Intro discount: 7% off list if contract signed within 14 days; additional professional services credits for an onboarding milestone (6-week adoption check).

ROI bullets (one-pager in the PDF):
- Estimated per-token cost reduction: 18% vs current pay-as-you-go profile at observed volumes.
- Expected monthly savings starting month 2 once recommender stabilizes: ~$3.4k on current run-rate.

Rishi: network/latency notes — we verified p95 < 180ms in us-west for similar recommender traffic at 1.5x current QPS during a 48-hour replay. I'm summarizing diagnostic traces in the PDF.

Next steps:
1) Amy/procurement: confirm acceptance of Tier B + burst bucket size.
2) If yes, Ben will issue a DocuSign with the pricing/rate table and the special terms.

Thanks — happy to hop on a 30-min call if you prefer to walk the execs through the ROI slide.

Laura Bennett
Renewals Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

title: Dedicated capacity options, surge lanes & phased activation for Theracore
preview:
From: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@theracore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity options — initial constraints

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re narrowing in on dedicated capacity for Theracore’s inference workloads (diagnostic model inference + realtime summarization for clinicians). Before we pull procurement in, can you send the concrete options you typically propose for cluster sizing, minimum GPU commitments, and a realistic ramp schedule?

A few hard constraints on our side:
- We need HIPAA-compliant deployment and an SLA with a 99.9% uptime target.
- Initial budget guidance is tight for Q2 — we could commit to an annual contract if the economics make sense.
- We expect spiky traffic around NPI launches (30–40% daily burst windows).

Specific asks:
1) 3–4 capacity options with estimated monthly committed spend and burst/buffer approach
2) Minimum guaranteed GPUs and recommended warmup/validation windows
3) Onboarding / ramp timeline (how quickly you can activate, warm caches, and reach steady throughput)

Can you get something over by early next week? Procurement will want a clear path (numbers + timeline).

Best,
Sofia Ramos
Head of Cloud Ops, Theracore

---
Attachment: request-constraints-theracore.pdf (1 page)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@theracore.ai>
Cc: Theracore Procurement <procurement@theracore.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options — initial constraints

Hi Sofia — thanks for the clear constraints. Attached is a short options pack and an onboarding worksheet that maps cluster sizing to ramp cadence. High-level summary below; full numbers and pricing in the PDF and workbook attached.

Overview (three recommended paths):

1) "Conservative Start" — Proof-of-value lane
- Commitment: 16 GPUs (NVIDIA A800-equivalent slots)
- Ramp: activation in 7–10 days, steady-state by week 3
- Burst approach: small cross-customer burst pool + soft-queueing, up to 2x short bursts
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $26k–$30k
- When to pick: limited budget, want low risk and fast validation

2) "Standard Production" — balanced throughput + economics
- Commitment: 64 GPUs baseline (can be licensed as 48 min guarantee + auto-scale to 64)
- Ramp: staged activation — 48 GPUs live day 1, 64 by day 21 (full traffic by day 30)
- Burst approach: dedicated burst lanes (reserved but billed on use) + cache warmup credits
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $95k–$110k (volume pricing available on annual commit)
- When to pick: production SLAs, predictable latency, and cost-efficiency at scale

3) "Enterprise Resilience" — high-availability + geo-redundancy
- Commitment: 128 GPUs with cross-region failover and separate warm pools
- Ramp: 6–8 week full activation (can phase regionally)
- Burst approach: guaranteed burst capacity + priority routing and fallback model variants
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $180k+
- When to pick: enterprise SLAs, regulatory isolation, large spike windows

Key contract terms and operational notes:
- Minimum guaranteed GPU clause: we recommend a 30-day min guarantee for the first tranche with a 30% ramp tolerance window to accommodate onboarding fluctuations.
- Warmup credits: included (equivalent to ~48 hours of peak throughput) to pre-fill KV caches and avoid cold-start latencies.
- Activation tasks: canary rollout, perf validation runbook, and auto-fallback policy to a compatible lower-capacity model for safety.
- SLA: 99.9% target available on Dedicated offers; enterprise SLA addendum for 99.95% with additional observability retainer.

Attachments: Theracore_Dedicated_Options_20260416.pdf (proposals + pricing), Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (activation schedule + test plan)

Next steps: if you like the "Standard Production" path, I can draft a tailored commitment with a 10% volume discount for an annual contract and propose an activation calendar. Open to a short call with procurement + engineering to walk the workbook.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

---
Attachment: Theracore_Dedicated_Options_20260416.pdf (3 pages)
Attachment: Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (6 tabs)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sofia Ramos <s.ramos@theracore.ai>
Cc: Theracore Procurement <procurement@theracore.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity options — initial constraints

Hi Sofia — thanks for the clear constraints. Attached is a short options pack and an onboarding worksheet that maps cluster sizing to ramp cadence. High-level summary below; full numbers and pricing in the PDF and workbook attached.

Overview (three recommended paths):

1) "Conservative Start" — Proof-of-value lane
- Commitment: 16 GPUs (NVIDIA A800-equivalent slots)
- Ramp: activation in 7–10 days, steady-state by week 3
- Burst approach: small cross-customer burst pool + soft-queueing, up to 2x short bursts
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $26k–$30k
- When to pick: limited budget, want low risk and fast validation

2) "Standard Production" — balanced throughput + economics
- Commitment: 64 GPUs baseline (can be licensed as 48 min guarantee + auto-scale to 64)
- Ramp: staged activation — 48 GPUs live day 1, 64 by day 21 (full traffic by day 30)
- Burst approach: dedicated burst lanes (reserved but billed on use) + cache warmup credits
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $95k–$110k (volume pricing available on annual commit)
- When to pick: production SLAs, predictable latency, and cost-efficiency at scale

3) "Enterprise Resilience" — high-availability + geo-redundancy
- Commitment: 128 GPUs with cross-region failover and separate warm pools
- Ramp: 6–8 week full activation (can phase regionally)
- Burst approach: guaranteed burst capacity + priority routing and fallback model variants
- Typical monthly committed spend (est): $180k+
- When to pick: enterprise SLAs, regulatory isolation, large spike windows

Key contract terms and operational notes:
- Minimum guaranteed GPU clause: we recommend a 30-day min guarantee for the first tranche with a 30% ramp tolerance window to accommodate onboarding fluctuations.
- Warmup credits: included (equivalent to ~48 hours of peak throughput) to pre-fill KV caches and avoid cold-start latencies.
- Activation tasks: canary rollout, perf validation runbook, and auto-fallback policy to a compatible lower-capacity model for safety.
- SLA: 99.9% target available on Dedicated offers; enterprise SLA addendum for 99.95% with additional observability retainer.

Attachments: Theracore_Dedicated_Options_20260416.pdf (proposals + pricing), Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (activation schedule + test plan)

Next steps: if you like the "Standard Production" path, I can draft a tailored commitment with a 10% volume discount for an annual contract and propose an activation calendar. Open to a short call with procurement + engineering to walk the workbook.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachment: Theracore_Onramp_Worksheet.xlsx (6 tabs)

Subject: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our legal and procurement teams reviewed the MSA and order form and attached a redline with a few items we need clarity on before we can proceed: uptime measurement baseline, service credit formula, response times for Sev 1/2, and maintenance window carveouts.

Immediate asks:
- Can Redwood confirm how uptime will be measured (rolling windows, exclusion criteria)?
- We want a clear service credit schedule tied to percentage drops below the uptime target.
- Clarify notification requirements for scheduled maintenance and emergency security fixes.

I've attached our annotated MSA for reference (Catalyst_MSA_Redline_20260410.docx). Can we target a short call next week to walk through?

—Samir
Samir Patel
Director, Cloud Operations
Catalyst Cloud

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Samir — thanks for the redline and clear list. Looping in Laura (legal) and Marcus (ops) so we can cover contract and runbook implications. Quick notes before a call: 

- Uptime measurement: Redwood's default hosted SLA uses a rolling 30-day window measured against successful request rates at the API gateway level. We can provide daily availability reports and raw telemetry for auditing.
- SLO tiers: Standard (99.9% monthly) vs Dedicated (99.95% with uplift). We can discuss Dedicated for your production-critical routes.
- Service credits: typical cap is the lesser of 100% of monthly invoice or a fixed cap (we propose 50% of monthly invoice as an upper bound). Credits accrue on a per-incident/period basis.
- Response times: P1 (1 hour initial response, continuous engagement until mitigation), P2 (4 hours initial).
- Maintenance notification: standard is 72-hour notice for scheduled maintenance; emergency security patches can be invoked with as little as 24-hour notice but include a rollback plan and post-mortem.

Propose a 30-min working session on Tue, Apr 13 at 10am PT to walk the redline and record agreed edits live. Attaching our initial order-form SLA proposal (RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf) and will bring a mutual action plan.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Account Exec — Strategic Customers
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf (pdf, 84 KB)

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — proposed legal positions (SLA & support)

Hi Samir / Ben,

I've reviewed Catalyst's redline and noted the key legal asks. High-level positions from Redwood Legal below — we can kill or accept specific language on the call but want to be explicit: 

1) Measurement window and exclusions
- We accept a rolling 30-day measurement window.
- Exclusions: agreed scheduled maintenance (with notice), force majeure, customer-caused incidents (e.g., misconfiguration), and third-party dependency outages we cannot reasonably control. We will provide telemetry to demonstrate exclusions.

2) Service credit formula
- We propose a stepped credit schedule (example: 0.5% credit for 99.5–99.74, 1.5% for 99.0–99.49, etc.) with a monthly cap equal to the monthly invoice for the affected services. We cannot accept an unlimited cumulative credit that exceeds the monthly invoice.

3) Severity and response times
- P1: 1-hour acknowledgement, 4-hour mitigation target (continuous communication).
- P2: 4-hour acknowledgement, 24-hour mitigation target.
- We can tighten for Dedicated if Catalyst commits to committed capacity.

4) Maintenance windows / emergency patches
- Scheduled: 72-hour notice, communicated via support portal + email.
- Emergency security patch: Redwood will aim for 24-hour notice where possible, but language needs to allow immediate deploys if an active exploitation is observed; we will provide a post-deploy report within 48 hours.

I've attached a redlined Order Form that marks suggested legal language. If Catalyst insists on a higher uptime band without Dedicated capacity and price uplift, that will be a commercial ask.

—Laura
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Privacy
Redwood Inference

Attachment: RDW_OrderForm_SLA_Proposal_20260412.pdf (annotated PDF, 92 KB)

On Mon, Apr 12, 2026 at 08:22 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Samir — thanks for the redline... (see above)

From: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>, legal@catalystcloud.com
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2026 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MSA redlines — SLA / support items to discuss

Thanks Laura and Ben — appreciate the clarity. A few follow-ups and counterpoints from our side after internal review: 

- Measurement & credits: Catalyst is aiming for 99.95% across our production tenancy. Our procurement team is prepared to accept a stepped credit schedule, but the credit quantum needs to be material: 5% of monthly fees per 0.1% below target up to 50% as a cap. We view this as a business continuity guarantee.
- Emergency patches: we understand the need for immediate remediation for active exploits, but we need a written escalation and canary plan for any urgent deploys into production. Unannounced disruptive changes are a showstopper.
- Evidence: we will require daily availability reports + access to a defined set of telemetry endpoints for a 30-day lookback during negotiation and a read-only data export thereafter for audit.

We can discuss on the call, but if Redwood is unwilling to move on the credit quantums, we will escalate procurement approvals.

—Samir

On Tue, Apr 10, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Samir — thanks for the redline and clear list...

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@catalystcloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Hannah Price <hannah.price@procure-catalyst.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Catalyst Cloud — MSA / Order Form redlines (SLA & support)

Samir — thanks for the direct feedback and for laying out Catalyst's internal threshold. After conferring with Legal and Ops, here's a proposed compromise that should be pathable without restructuring our commercial model: 

Summary of compromise proposal:
- Uptime: 99.90% standard for hosted plans; 99.95% available for Dedicated (requires 1.25x committed capacity uplift).
- Service credit schedule: stepped credits tied to availability bands; example (monthly): 99.90–99.74 = 5% credit; 99.69–99.49 = 15%; <99.49 = 30%, with a hard cap at 50% of the monthly invoice for the affected services. We will provide a worked example on the call.
- Measurement & evidence: rolling 30-day window; Redwood will deliver daily availability reports and grant a read-only telemetry export for a 30-day rolling period to facilitate audits.
- Emergency maintenance: 24-hour notice for security patches where feasible; immediate deploys permitted only if active exploitation is detected and the only reasonable mitigation is deploy. In such events Redwood will: (a) notify within 4 hours, (b) run a canary deployment for 30 minutes, (c) provide a post-deploy incident report within 48 hours.
- Response targets: P1 acknowledgement 1 hour; target mitigation within 4 hours with continuous updates. P2 acknowledgement 4 hours, mitigation target 24 hours.

Next steps
- If this framework is acceptable in principle, propose a 60-minute redline session Fri, Apr 16 at 9am PT (Ben/Laura/Marcus + Samir/Hannah + Catalyst legal).
- I'll circulate a Mutual Action Plan with dates and responsibility owners and attach a DocuSign link for the Order Form once redlines are agreed.

Attaching: Catalyst-MutualActionPlan-20260415.xlsx (mutual action plan with milestones). DocuSign sample link: https://docusign.com/XYZ-Agreement-1938

If we can agree on the compromise, we should be able to get legal sign-off + procurement approval within 2–3 business days after the redline session.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Exec — Strategic Customers
Redwood Inference

- Evidence: we will require daily availability reports + access to a defined set of telemetry endpoints for a 30-day lookback during negotiation and a read-only data export thereafter for audit.

We can discuss on the call, but if Redwood is unwilling to move on the credit quantums, we will escalate procurement approvals.

—Samir

- Response targets: P1 acknowledgement 1 hour; target mitigation within 4 hours with continuous updates. P2 acknowledgement 4 hours, mitigation target 24 hours.

title: Re: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan for NovaPay
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Alex — thanks for the time this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side so we have a shared acceptance bar for the pilot. Please review and flag anything you want adjusted.

Summary of scope
- Feature: real-time customer dispute summarization and suggested first-response snippets (integration to NovaPay support UI).
- Duration: 3 weeks active evaluation + 1 week validation (4 weeks total).
- Traffic pattern for test: replay of 10k anonymized support threads (provided by NovaPay), plus 100 live-monitoring requests/day for latency sampling.

Proposed success criteria (objective metrics)
- Latency: p95 end-to-end < 250 ms for single-request summarization under pilot throughput.
- Throughput: sustained 60 req/s for a 5-minute window (burst to 120 req/s) with <1% error rate.
- Cost: average cost/token <= $0.0006 (estimated token mix attached).
- Quality: automated QA using your gold set — 85% pass on rubric (coverage, factuality, actionable suggestion). Manual audit on 200 samples: target >= 88% accept.
- Safety: hallucination rate <2% on audited samples; no PII leakage in outputs.

Operational items / ownership
- NovaPay data owner & POC: Alex Johnson (will assign Sofia as engineering POC).
- Redwood PoC lead: Aditya Rao; Eng support: Ben Carter; Success manager: Marissa Cole.
- Deliverables: evaluation dataset onboarding, test harness (we provide), latency/throughput runbook, weekly syncs.

Timeline (proposed)
- Week 0 (this week): NDA/SOW sign, dataset handoff sample (1k rows).
- Week 1: ingest & baseline runs, initial latency profile, make tuning recommendations.
- Week 2: throughput stress runs + batch tuning; begin quality evaluations.
- Week 3: finalize quality tuning & run acceptance tests.
- Week 4: validation window and acceptance decision.

Next steps (actionable)
1) Alex — confirm dataset delivery timeline and point person for sanitized export.
2) Alex — confirm acceptance threshold adjustments (if any).
3) Redwood — send SOW draft, security checklist (SOC2/retention), and a small sample eval set we will use.

Attachments: latency-throughput-plan.xlsx (test matrix), novapay-eval-dataset-sample.csv (30 rows), novapay-pilot-sow-v1.pdf (draft).

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Sofia Martins <sofia.martins@novapay.com>
Date: 2026-04-12T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot success metrics & evaluation plan follow-up

Aditya — this is a great summary, thanks. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Throughput: we actually expect peak support hours to require ~100 req/s sustained for the pilot window. Can Redwood confirm platform can sustain 100 req/s with p95 under 300 ms? If so we can accept the latency target at p95 <300 ms given the higher throughput.

2) Quality: our gold set will be 500 labeled threads (we'll share a 50-row sample first). For manual audit please plan for random sampling of 300 items rather than 200.

3) Security & compliance: procurement will need SOC2 Type 2 and our vendor assessment completed before production rollout. For the pilot we need a short security attestation and a data retention plan for the test dataset.

I've added Sofia (engineer) to own the dataset export and point her to Ben for integration details. Procurement will loop in their channel separately.

If Redwood can push a tightened SOW (with 100 req/s target and the alternative latency band) today, Sofia and I will review and aim to hand off the sanitized dataset by EOD Friday (2026-04-16).

Best,
Alex
Head of Support Automation — NovaPay
alex.johnson@novapay.com

To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Alex Johnson <alex.johnson@novapay.com>

Subject: Fwd: Vendor onboarding checklist / SOW request

Forwarding our vendor onboarding requirements. Aditya — procurement needs:
- Completed vendor security questionnaire (we can start with your SOC2 summary).
- Data retention policy for pilot artifacts (log retention, dataset deletion window).
- Template SOW with pricing caps and termination clauses (pilot-specific).

Can you send the required docs and indicate an expected procurement lead time if SOW is signed this week? We typically take 7–10 business days for standard review; expedited review possible with legal signoff.

title: Renewal: onboarding acceleration + staggered PO for capacity uplift
preview:
From: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2026 14:02:00 -0400
Subject: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Hi Aditya / team,

Quick context — Globex's renewal is coming up (contract ends 2026-06-30). Procurement asked me to see if we can accelerate vendor re-onboarding so we can finalize a staggered PO (initial commit + top-up tranche) before the end of May. Two asks from our side: 1) confirm process for "vendor re-certification" in your portal (we need to upload SOC2 artifacts and new W-9), and 2) provide a short mutual action plan so legal/finance can sign off on the split PO approach.

We'd like:
- a checklist for what PR/Finance needs to approve the staggered PO
- expected turnaround time if we submit docs this week
- single-person contact who can shepherd the vendor onboarding steps

I can loop internal finance (cc'd) once we have the checklist. Appreciate a crisp plan — Globex is tying seat increase (from 200k to 350k tokens/month equivalent) to the renewal.

Thanks,
Lori Chen
Procurement Lead, Globex Corp

--
Attachment: procurement_request_form.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Apr 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Lori — thanks for the clear ask. High-level proposal below and attaching a one-page mutual action plan to make procurement/finance signoff straightforward.

Summary of path:
1) Vendor re-onboarding in Redwood Procurement Portal (link below) — requires SOC2 evidence index, updated PO/POB (purchase order/billing contact), and updated tax docs. Portal submission typically validates-in 48-72 hours for basic docs; evidence review for SOC2 artifacts can add 3-5 business days.
2) Staggered PO approach — we can accept an initial PO for 60% of committed seats and a top-up PO for the remainder to be issued within 90 days of renewal start. Legal has pre-approved this pattern for customers of your size (we'll attach the SOW addendum).
3) Single point liaison — Jordan will own the portal handoff and will be the escalation for any evidence gaps. Marissa will oversee finance/legal coordination for the PO split. Evelyn will track SOC2 evidence items and confirm the evidence index.

Immediate next steps I suggest (mutual action plan):
- Today: Lori to confirm procurement email and attach the initial PO draft + W-9 (if available).
- By 14 Apr: Jordan will create vendor profile in portal and send portal invite to Globex procurement.
- By 16 Apr: Globex uploads SOC2 evidence index + any requested artifacts.
- By 20 Apr: Redwood validates the evidence index and sends SOW addendum (redlines allowed via DocuSign).
- By 28 Apr: Finance signoff on initial PO (60%) and DocuSign complete.

Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx; I'll also include the SOW redlines after legal finalizes.

If this timeline works, Jordan will start the portal invite tomorrow morning.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Exec — Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Hi Lori — I'll own the portal steps. Quick notes on the onboarding path and a couple of friction points we've seen: 

Portal steps (short):
- Create vendor profile -> assign procurement user -> upload tax docs -> submit evidence index -> vendor attestation -> finance PO attach.

Common friction and mitigations:
- SOC2 evidence scanned as a single PDF can cause field-mapping delays; please use the evidence index CSV in the attachment so we can map to our portal fields.
- If procurement can't upload the full SOC2 report due to NDAs, a signed summary + control mapping is sufficient for the onboarding gate; we'll list what we need in the evidence index.

DocuSign flow: our legal prefers the SOW addendum executed electronically. I'll prepare a DocuSign envelope and share the link as soon as legal finishes redlines.

Portal invite will come from onboarding@redwood.com — keep an eye on spam folders and whitelist that domain. I plan to send the invite later today (14 Apr).

Jordan
Implementation Lead, Customer Onboarding
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: SOC2_evidence_index.csv

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>

Date: Tue, 14 Apr 2026 16:20:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Lori — looping in Finance (Evelyn) explicitly to confirm PO split approvals and billing schedule. Evelyn, see brief below and doc links (HubSpot deal GLX-089). We are ok to proceed with the initial 60% PO + top-up if the billing/recognition cadence works for Globex. Please confirm EOM billing cadence, and whether you need the vendor invoice to include both POs or the initial only.

Also, legal asked me to flag a single minor redline on the SOW addendum (term change clause): they want an explicit rollback clause if usage exceeds the 90-day ramp by >30% so we can auto-trigger a capacity reprice review. I'll attach our redline PDF.

If everything is green, Aditya — please push the DocuSign envelope today so Lori can sign and procurement can upload docs.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Director, Commercial Operations
Redwood Inference

--

title: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive
preview:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
To: Alex Murray <amurray@novacorp.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@novacorp.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Hi Alex / Priya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap: Novacorp is prioritizing a live technical deep dive to validate inference throughput for your mixed-traffic (short chat + long-form summarization) use cases, and you asked for a schedule that rotates across US/EU/APAC-friendly times so product teams in each region can join periodically.

Rather than a single fixed time, we often run a 3-band "Commuter Bands" approach that alternates who gets the most convenient slot on a weekly basis. It looks like this (example):

- Band A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 PT / 13:00 ET / Wed 02:00 SGT
- Band B (EU-friendly): Wed 15:00 CET / 09:00 ET / Thu 00:00 SGT
- Band C (APAC-friendly): Thu 08:00 SGT / Wed 20:00 CET / Wed 11:00 PT

Benefits: equitable engineer attendance, predictable calendar footprint, and better overlap for cross-region Q&A.

Request: please indicate which of the three bands you prefer for the first session next week and who from your infra/product team needs to attend. I attached a draft mutual action plan (NOVA-Mutual-Action-Plan.xlsx) and an .ics with the three sample windows.

If it's easier, here's a Calendly link with the three slots pre-populated: https://calendly.com/redwood-inference/technical-deep-dive — pick a band and we'll lock in a recurring cadence (weekly or biweekly).

Next steps on our side: I'll loop in Ben (sales engineering) to align on the demo scope and confirm a small dataset we can load beforehand.

Thanks — look forward to syncing.

Best,
Soojin Lee
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
soojin@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments: NOVA-Mutual-Action-Plan.xlsx (spreadsheet, 45 KB), commuter-band-availability.ics (calendar file)


From: Alex Murray <amurray@novacorp.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@novacorp.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2026 08:06:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Follow-up: Proposed 'Commuter Bands' for Multi-TZ Deep Dive

Thanks, Soojin —

Appreciate the clear options. Priya and I reviewed the MAP and the bands. For the kickoff next week we prefer Band B (EU-friendly) because our infra folks in London and Berlin can attend more reliably on Wednesdays.

Attendees from Novacorp: Alex Murray (platform lead), Priya Singh (product manager), Daniel Kwon (SRE) — Daniel is in Seoul so he can join at the late-night overlap occasionally but will likely be present for Band C sessions.

One small ask: can your demo include a short run showing tail-latency distribution for small (1–2 token) spammy requests vs longer summary requests? That will help Daniel validate the cache behavior.

I'll pick a slot via the Calendly link later today.

Best,
Alex
Alex Murray | Platform Lead, Novacorp
